W-TARGET-URGENT.plain_language: Ambitious timeline — living in the US within 6 months
W-TARGET-URGENT.why_it_matters: A 6-month target is achievable but leaves little room for delays. Starting preparation immediately gives you the best chance of meeting it.

W-AGING-OUT.plain_language: Some children aged 18–20 may age out before visa issuance
W-AGING-OUT.why_it_matters: Dependent status only applies to unmarried children under 21. Children aged 18–20 risk losing eligibility if the visa process takes longer than expected.

W-OVER-21.plain_language: Children 21 or older do not qualify as E-2 dependents
W-OVER-21.why_it_matters: Adult children over 21 cannot be included on your E-2 application. They will need their own visa pathway (such as an independent E-2, H-1B, or student visa).

W-PARTNERSHIP-SPLIT.why_it_matters: Unequal splits or silent investors raise questions about who controls the business. E-2 requires the principal applicant to own at least 50% and actively manage the enterprise.

W-FAMILY-GIFT.plain_language: Investment funded in part by a gift or inheritance
W-FAMILY-GIFT.why_it_matters: Gift-funded investments require documentation proving the gift is genuine and not a disguised loan. You will need a gift letter and evidence of the donor's ability to give.

W-LOAN-FUNDED.plain_language: Investment funded in part by a loan
W-LOAN-FUNDED.why_it_matters: Loan-funded investments require careful structuring. The loan must be secured by your personal assets, not the business itself. Attorney guidance is recommended for this path.

W-LOW-INVESTMENT.plain_language: Investment between $75,000 and $100,000 — higher scrutiny expected
W-LOW-INVESTMENT.why_it_matters: The standard substantiality benchmark is $100,000+. Below that, you will need to demonstrate proportionality more aggressively relative to total business cost.

W-BORDERLINE-INVESTMENT.plain_language: Investment between $50,000 and $75,000 — below typical threshold
W-BORDERLINE-INVESTMENT.why_it_matters: This range is below the usual substantiality threshold. Success requires strong justification that your investment is proportionate to the total cost of the business.

W-NO-BIZ-IDENTIFIED.why_it_matters: A business must be identified before an E-2 application can be filed. Preparation can begin now — building a strong profile while you search will save time later.

PR-PASSIVE-INVEST.plain_language: Passive real estate holdings generally do not qualify for E-2
PR-PASSIVE-INVEST.why_it_matters: The E-2 visa requires an active, operating commercial enterprise. Passive rental properties or investment portfolios held for income do not meet this requirement.

PR-NONPROFIT.plain_language: Non-profit organisations do not qualify for E-2 classification
PR-NONPROFIT.why_it_matters: The E-2 Treaty Investor visa requires a for-profit commercial enterprise. Non-profit organisations are not eligible.

PR-LOW-INVESTMENT.plain_language: Investment under $50,000 — unlikely to meet substantiality requirement
PR-LOW-INVESTMENT.why_it_matters: Investments below $50,000 rarely meet the substantiality requirement for E-2 classification. The treaty investor visa requires an investment that is substantial relative to the total cost of the enterprise.

W-REFUSAL-RECENT.plain_language: Prior US visa refusal within the last 5 years
W-REFUSAL-RECENT.why_it_matters: Recent refusals must be addressed in your application narrative. You will need to explain what has changed since the refusal and why your circumstances are now different.

W-REFUSAL-OLD.plain_language: US visa refusal more than 5 years ago
W-REFUSAL-OLD.why_it_matters: Older refusals still need to be disclosed on your application, but they carry less weight. A brief explanation of the circumstances is usually sufficient.

W-REFUSAL-MULTIPLE.plain_language: Multiple US visa refusals on record
W-REFUSAL-MULTIPLE.why_it_matters: Multiple refusals create a pattern that requires strong evidence of eligibility and changed circumstances. This will need careful narrative preparation.

W-ENTRY-REFUSED.plain_language: Refused entry at a US border
W-ENTRY-REFUSED.why_it_matters: Border refusals are more serious than visa refusals and must be thoroughly addressed. This creates a record with CBP that requires careful legal navigation.

W-DEPORTED.plain_language: Prior deportation or removal from the US
W-DEPORTED.why_it_matters: Deportation or removal orders create significant legal complexity, including potential bars on re-entry. Professional legal guidance is essential before proceeding.

W-CONVICTION-RECENT.plain_language: Criminal conviction within the last 10 years or serious offence
W-CONVICTION-RECENT.why_it_matters: Recent or serious criminal convictions require careful admissibility analysis. Some convictions create automatic bars to entry. Consulting an immigration attorney is strongly recommended.

W-CONVICTION-OLD.plain_language: Minor criminal conviction resolved more than 10 years ago
W-CONVICTION-OLD.why_it_matters: Older minor convictions are generally less problematic but still require disclosure. Documentation of the conviction and evidence of resolution should be prepared.

W-CONVICTION-UNSURE.why_it_matters: Uncertainty about your criminal record requires professional legal review before filing. An attorney can help obtain records and assess admissibility.

W-WEAK-TIES.plain_language: Limited ties to your home country
W-WEAK-TIES.why_it_matters: E-2 applicants must demonstrate non-immigrant intent — that they plan to return home. Limited ties make this harder to prove and may require additional supporting evidence.

W-PROP-STRONG-30.why_it_matters: Your investment represents less than 30% of the total business cost. USCIS evaluates whether the investment is substantial relative to the enterprise. A low ratio may weaken the substantiality argument.

W-PROP-SOFT-30.plain_language: Investment-to-cost ratio between 30% and 50% — moderate proportionality risk
W-PROP-SOFT-30.why_it_matters: Your investment represents 30–50% of the total business cost. While not disqualifying, a higher ratio strengthens the substantiality argument. Consider whether increasing the investment is feasible.

PR-NON-TREATY.plain_language: Country does not have an active E-2 treaty with the US
PR-NON-TREATY.why_it_matters: Only nationals of countries with a Treaty of Commerce and Navigation with the United States are eligible for E-2 classification. This is a hard eligibility requirement.
